domain: Berber tifinagh consonantal abjad (Tamazight/Berber script; consonant-only writing with vowels reader-supplied except word-final; deliberately incomplete encoding; ~30% character reduction)
specific_mechanism: Deliberately incomplete encoding — only consonants written, vowels reconstructed by reader from linguistic context.
llm_application: TIFINAGH-SPARSE-TOK: a deliberately-incomplete tokenizer for low-resource languages. Drop a fraction (configurable, default ~30%) of HIGH-FREQUENCY non-content tokens (functional analog of vowels — e.g., common particles, function words, fillers) from the tokenizer's vocabulary. At inference, the LLM's contextual completion fills the omitted tokens during decoding (with explicit GAP-MARKERS as placeholders that the model auto-fills). Token-count reduction → faster decoding. Trade-off: requires model with strong contextual completion + post-decoding gap-fill. Differs from BPE (full coverage), BoundlessBPE (boundary relaxation), Cherokee fixed-vocab (custom inventory) by EXPLICIT VOWEL-LIKE OMISSION + CONTEXT-FILL at inference time.
novelty_claim: BPE/SentencePiece/WordPiece all preserve full vocabulary. BoundlessBPE relaxes pre-tokenization boundaries. Language-specific tokenizers initialise primitives. TIFINAGH-SPARSE-TOK differs by (a) DELIBERATELY OMITTING a class of frequent tokens, (b) using GAP-MARKERS for omitted positions, (c) using LLM contextual completion as integrated reconstruction during decoding, (d) targeting token-count reduction for low-resource languages.
